focus: Club fitting transformation, senior distance loss, aging golfer struggles, shaft flex impact

PAIN.quote: Before Covid, before I discovered THP, back in the 2000s-to early 2010s, I played more often - took a break when I got really big, had some physical issues, etc. But 250 + yard drives with the equipment of the day was nothing spectacular - it was the norm for me. Ok, cut to the Covid era, I had lost close to 100 lbs, started playing golf again, had a bunch of newer equipment and distance was gone, I mean G-O-N-E gone
PAIN.source: Hackers Paradise - Golf and Aging and sudden loss of distance

PAIN.quote: In 2012, I holed two shots with a 52 degree wedge from 120 yards. Currently, the 48 degree GW is my 105 club. So it's been a considerable decline in 13 years
PAIN.source: Hackers Paradise - Golf and Aging and sudden loss of distance
PAIN.context: 15 yards per club lost in 13 years

PAIN.quote: I feel you, it seems once I hit like 40 my distance took a hit, 45 a bigger hit and 50 a pretty damn big one. I'm not ready to change tee boxes and just have grown accustomed to hitting a long iron/wood on my second shot
PAIN.source: Hackers Paradise - Golf and Aging and sudden loss of distance

PAIN.quote: I was struggling to get the ball airbourne with my Callaway FT-I before hand, the shaft was just dead to me. Even when I did get the ball airbourne it only went about 210 TOTAL distance
PAIN.source: Golf Monthly - Custom fit driver difference

PAIN.quote: The driver has always been one club that i've had problems with (who hasn't!) and i've never really been settled with any driver i've had
PAIN.source: Golf Monthly - Custom fit driver difference

PAIN.quote: I've only broken 90 twice. I feel that I'm currently not at a point in my game where I should splurge on getting fitted for a set
PAIN.source: GolfWRX - Did getting fitted make a huge difference?
PAIN.context: Feels not good enough for fitting

HOPE.quote: HUUUUUUGE difference to my game. Now with my C/F'd FT-5 and Aldila NVS shaft I am carrying about 220-230, on good days, my ball will roll out to 250
HOPE.source: Golf Monthly - Custom fit driver difference

HOPE.quote: I used to have a Nike Ignite 460 with regular shaft. Couldn't hit a fairway to save me life - now I'm averaging over 60%
HOPE.source: Golf Monthly - Custom fit driver difference

HOPE.quote: I had a couple of drivers custom fit and my last one has changed my driving tenfold. I have dropped the spin rate so I don't hit into the wind, I hit through it which is nice as I struggled with that before
HOPE.source: Golf Monthly - Custom fit driver difference

HOPE.quote: Yes it did. I couldn't hit driver before and the fitter found a club that worked with my swing. Even better, I learned what a club should feel like so I could shop better going forward
HOPE.source: GolfWRX - Did getting fitted make a huge difference?
HOPE.context: Couldn't hit driver - fitting fixed it

HOPE.quote: A proper fitting 100% has improved my game. The 2 things that were far and away the most important were the driver shaft fitting and the iron lie angle adjustment. If you have ANY consistency these will help
HOPE.source: GolfWRX - Did getting fitted make a huge difference?

HOPE.quote: Today, I tried with a high degree of uncertainty a Senior flex shaft - and for one round at least - distance - still not the stuff to write home about - but considerably longer and frankly, it was intoxicating
HOPE.source: Hackers Paradise - Golf and Aging and sudden loss of distance

HOPE.quote: I've just had a custom fit driver. I ended up with a 10.5 degree Ping Rapture V2 with a stiff Diamana Blue shaft. I increased my launch angle by 2 degrees and brought my spin rate down which resulted in an increase of 15 yards carry. I will never buy an off the shelf driver again
HOPE.source: Golf Monthly - Custom fit driver difference
HOPE.context: 15 yards carry gained - never off shelf again

ROOT_CAUSE.quote: I think there definitely comes a point where you just aren't going to hit it as far anymore. At 65, you are probably past this point, so yeah you have to deal with declining distance
ROOT_CAUSE.source: Hackers Paradise - Golf and Aging and sudden loss of distance

ROOT_CAUSE.quote: Distance in golf is more about technique without compensations. As we get older the compensations seem to multiply and make everything more difficult
ROOT_CAUSE.source: Hackers Paradise - Golf and Aging and sudden loss of distance

ROOT_CAUSE.quote: Custom Fit can't correct swing inadequacies, but it can help with swing traits, if that makes sense? ie, shaft fitted for slow or fast swing etc.? Loft tweaks to help you fly the ball better depending on your natural launch angle and/or sidespin
ROOT_CAUSE.source: Golf Monthly - Custom fit driver difference

ROOT_CAUSE.quote: Many of the recent drivers have ridiculously long shafts which may help with distance but make control a real problem. Since then have stuck with drivers with a 44 inch shaft and ignored those such as the Burner with 46 or even 47 inch shafts
ROOT_CAUSE.source: Golf Monthly - Custom fit driver difference

SOLUTIONS_TRIED.quote: One of the members here went for a fitting and the guy decided to try something unusual and put him in a very soft flex shaft. Less than senior flex and it is amazing to watch how much it moves in his swing. But, for Sam, because his timing is always great, it gave him yardage back because the head was whipping through the ball
SOLUTIONS_TRIED.source: Hackers Paradise - Golf and Aging and sudden loss of distance
SOLUTIONS_TRIED.context: Very soft flex = whipping through ball

SOLUTIONS_TRIED.quote: Slowed the pace a bit by doing core workouts to help build golf muscles and do speed training but it's more reduced the pace than reversed anything. Time remains undefeated
SOLUTIONS_TRIED.source: Hackers Paradise - Golf and Aging and sudden loss of distance

SOLUTIONS_TRIED.quote: I also struggled with shots to the left but I am finding fairways more often and I can go 'after it' more often not in fear of losing it left
SOLUTIONS_TRIED.source: Golf Monthly - Custom fit driver difference
SOLUTIONS_TRIED.context: Fitting removed fear of going after it

SOLUTIONS_TRIED.quote: He agreed that it didn't make much sense to get fitted as I didn't have a repeatable swing... Well, with these irons I'm an entirely different golfer. My swing feels far more natural, I'm able to generate a ton more speed naturally, and I'm able to deliver the face to the ball square, right in the middle of the sweet spot far more often than not
SOLUTIONS_TRIED.source: GolfWRX - Did getting fitted make a huge difference?
SOLUTIONS_TRIED.context: Was told swing not repeatable - different golfer with right clubs

COMPETITOR_MECHANISM.quote: The driver shaft fitting was great for me. For about $100 my dispersion really tightened up
COMPETITOR_MECHANISM.source: GolfWRX - Did getting fitted make a huge difference?

COMPETITOR_MECHANISM.quote: Getting shorter doesn't mean getting worse, necessarily. One of my friends who is 80 now, played to a 'plus' handicap into his early 70s and finished top 10 in our CC until he was 75-76 or so
COMPETITOR_MECHANISM.source: Hackers Paradise - Golf and Aging and sudden loss of distance

COMPETITOR_MECHANISM.quote: The shaft (and possibly the lie angle - I hit these 3 degrees toe up vs 2 degrees toe down with mine) completely transformed my swing. I played a sim golf round with them and scored 3 strokes better than my buddy who has a handicap 14 strokes lower than mine
COMPETITOR_MECHANISM.source: GolfWRX - Did getting fitted make a huge difference?

VILLAIN.quote: I was getting out driven by a kid - seemed to be late 20s, who was just drilling his driving iron. It was a bit of a letdown but also fun to witness, he did not hit driver one time today. I hit driver on every par 4 and 5
VILLAIN.source: Hackers Paradise - Golf and Aging and sudden loss of distance

VILLAIN.quote: I'm a HUGE sceptic about the whole fitting of clubs thing - you still have to put a good swing on it regardless of what's in your hand
VILLAIN.source: Golf Monthly - Custom fit driver difference

VILLAIN.quote: I need to stop chasing distance that I just may not have the ability to catch any longer and work instead on better ball striking, more consistent ball striking
VILLAIN.source: Hackers Paradise - Golf and Aging and sudden loss of distance

VILLAIN.quote: I feel the move forward to the senior tees is about distance, not age
VILLAIN.source: Hackers Paradise - Golf and Aging and sudden loss of distance
VILLAIN.context: Senior tees about distance not age

sources_covered: Hackers Paradise - The Taboo Thread Golf and Aging
Golf Monthly - How much difference has a custom fit driver made
GolfWRX - Did getting fitted for clubs make a huge difference
high_value_themes_identified: 15 yards per club lost in 13 years
